preamble: The velar nasal /ŋ/ can undergo deletion, resulting in its complete loss. This phonetic transformation often occurs in specific environments, such as word-finally, intervocalically, or in certain morphological contexts.

examples.note: 'to stuff' (word-final velar nasal loss with vowel nasalization)
'beer' (word-final velar nasal loss with vowel nasalization)
'price' (word-final velar nasal loss with vowel nasalization)
'to practice' (word-final velar nasal loss with vowel nasalization)

sources: The consonants of proto-upper cross and their implications for the classification of the upper cross languages. ResearchGate.
Hogan, L. C. (1994). Nasalization in Lhasa Tibetan. Linguistics of the Tibeto-Burman Area, 17(2).
Marley, A. (2022). Sound change in Aboriginal Australia: Word-initial engma deletion in Kunwok. Linguistics Vanguard, 1-23.
The fate of the Benue-Congo velar nasal in Bantoid. AFRIKA UND ÜBERSEE.
